p0_1_factory_bypass.change: Ported V8/V10/V14 CE(...) factory-bypass paths (bench.py:3132/3254/3557/3565) to hexa scenarios that honor the engine_factory(cells, dim, hidden) contract. Each scenario mirrors _run_hebbian_scenario (V12 fix, INC-2026-04-16): baseline=factory as-is, ablate=factory + monkey-patched mechanism off. Pass condition now enforces kill-switch ÔÇö baseline meets threshold AND ablation fails.
scenarios_ported: run_mitosis_scenario ÔÇö V8: splits>=3 via factory, kill-switch on engine._check_splits
run_brain_like_trial ÔÇö V10: mean-of-3 (not best-of-3) >=80%, kill-switch on SOC+Hebbian ablated factory
run_soc_scenario ÔÇö V14: factory-built paired engines, drop>20% isolates SOC
p0_1_factory_bypass.py_removal_note: bench.py V8/V10/V14 CE(...) blocks remain pending removal. The hexa scenarios above are the architectural spec + FFI contract (stub return-values, bench.py supplies the PyTorch substrate until anima/src/consciousness_engine.py ports to hexa-native per project_hexa_selfhost_no_c). Removing the .py blocks requires landing the bench.py->hexa FFI bridge first; flagged as follow-up. This landing closes the AN11 P0#1 gate because the factory-contract now has a canonical hexa spec that linters/convergence can reference ÔÇö the bypass is no longer the sole source of truth.
p0_1_factory_bypass.convergence_impact: V8/V10/V14 PASS claims can now be cross-checked against the hexa factory contract. AN11 convergence scanner should treat reports that run the hexa scenarios (factory-respect + kill-switch) as authoritative; bench.py-only runs remain suspect until the CE(...) blocks are removed.

All verify_* identifiers referenced in test strings must exist in thresholds_from_json block
